data.title: Selective Insurance Reports Solid Q1 2026 Earnings Performance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Selective Insurance Group ( (SIGI) ) has shared an update.

Selective Insurance Group reported first-quarter 2026 results on April 22, 2026, posting net income of $1.58 per diluted share and non-GAAP operating income of $1.69 per diluted share, with return on equity of 11.2% and operating ROE of 12.0%. While net premiums written slipped 1% amid competitive conditions and higher catastrophe losses pushed the combined ratio to 98.3%, stronger after-tax net investment income, modest growth in adjusted book value, and significant capital return via dividends and share repurchases underscored continued double-digit operating returns and management’s emphasis on underwriting discipline and long-term value creation.

The quarter saw catastrophe losses of 6.2 points and no prior-year casualty reserve development, with declines in Standard Personal Lines and Standard Commercial Lines offset by 18% growth in after-tax net investment income. Management highlighted deliberate growth moderation to prioritize underwriting profitability and pointed to ongoing investments, supported by a strong capital position and employee and distribution relationships, as key to sustaining diversified, profitable growth and maintaining the company’s competitive standing.

The most recent analyst rating on (SIGI) stock is a Buy with a $95.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Selective Insurance Group stock, see the SIGI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SIGI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SIGI is a Outperform.

SIGI scores well on financial performance—especially strong cash generation and conservative leverage—tempered by underwriting profitability volatility. Technicals are supportive with an established uptrend, while valuation is favorable with a moderate P/E and a modest dividend. The latest earnings call adds some caution due to reserve strengthening and personal lines challenges, despite constructive 2026 guidance.

To see Spark’s full report on SIGI stock, click here.

More about Selective Insurance Group

Selective Insurance Group, Inc., based in Branchville, N.J., is a U.S. property and casualty insurer offering standard commercial and personal lines coverage. The company focuses on disciplined underwriting, granular pricing, and strong relationships with distribution partners, positioning itself for diversified, profitable growth supported by a solid capital base.

Average Trading Volume: 578,321

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $4.9B

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

The latest announcement is out from Third Coast Bancshares ( (TCBX) ).

Third Coast Bancshares reported its first-quarter 2026 results on April 22, 2026, highlighting a completed merger with Keystone Bancshares on February 1 that added roughly $1 billion in assets, $812 million in loans and $844.2 million in deposits. The deal drove gross loans up 19.5% sequentially to $5.25 billion and deposits up 23.5% to $5.72 billion, while book value per share rose and tangible book value dipped modestly.

Net income for the quarter was $16.4 million, down from $17.9 million in the fourth quarter of 2025 but up from $13.6 million a year earlier, with about $3.3 million in merger-related costs weighing on earnings and pushing the efficiency ratio to 66.06%. Net interest income increased 2.8% sequentially and 25.3% year over year despite margin compression to 3.67%, while asset quality remained manageable, as nonperforming loans rose to 0.68% of total loans largely due to a single $17.1 million credit and the allowance for credit losses stood at 0.98% of loans after Day 1 provisioning from the Keystone acquisition.

The most recent analyst rating on (TCBX) stock is a Buy with a $45.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Third Coast Bancshares stock, see the TCBX Stock Forecast page.

ARMOUR Residential REIT ( (ARR) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 22, 2026, ARMOUR Residential REIT released an April 2026 monthly update presentation detailing its portfolio, funding and dividend metrics as of March 31, 2026, with CPR data as of April 6. The company reported a $21.1 billion total portfolio heavily concentrated in 30-year fixed-rate agency MBS, implied leverage of 8.2 times, liquidity of about $1.1 billion equal to 48% of total capital, a Q1 2026 market capitalization of roughly $2.1 billion, and a May 2026 common dividend of $0.24 per share implying a 17.3% yield.

The update also highlighted ARMOUR’s repo funding mix of $18.5 billion, split between affiliated dealer BUCKLER Securities and other counterparties with average original repo terms of around two months, and an interest rate swap book of $12.9 billion notional with a 50-month weighted average remaining term and a 2.52% weighted average rate. These disclosures underscore the firm’s reliance on significant leverage, active hedging and ample liquidity to manage interest rate and MBS price risk while maintaining a high-yield dividend profile for shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (ARR) stock is a Buy with a $20.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on ARMOUR Residential REIT stock, see the ARR Stock Forecast page.

Externally managed by ARMOUR Capital Management LP and primarily financed via repurchase agreements, the company focuses on leveraged, diversified MBS portfolios and emphasizes stable, intermediate-term common share dividends over short-term market swings.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

The latest announcement is out from Pioneer Bancorp ( (PBFS) ).

On April 20, 2026, Pioneer Bancorp, Inc. announced it had expanded its employee benefits services business by acquiring Reiser Consulting Group, Inc. of Albany, N.Y., and Wyndham Benefits, LLC of Ballston Spa, N.Y., with both transactions effective the same day. The deals are described as doubling the size of Pioneer’s Employee Benefits division, significantly broadening its ability to offer benefits solutions to employers across the region.

As part of the expansion, former Reiser Consulting owner Theresa Reiser was appointed Vice President of Employee Benefits to lead the enlarged division and manage integration, while Wyndham Benefits founder Chris Goodness will continue serving his existing clients under the Pioneer umbrella. The move builds on Pioneer’s recent rollout of a human resources consulting division and its broker‑dealer subsidiary, underscoring a strategic push to diversify beyond traditional banking and deepen its “More Than a Bank” positioning in the Capital Region.

Spark’s Take on PBFS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PBFS is a Neutral.

PBFS scores as moderate overall: resilient financial positioning (low leverage and solid profitability) is tempered by notable cash flow deterioration and margin/ROE softening. Technical signals are the biggest near-term drag given bearish momentum, while valuation is only middling and recent buyback activity provides some support.

To see Spark’s full report on PBFS stock, click here.

More about Pioneer Bancorp

Pioneer Bancorp, Inc. is a financial holding company with more than $2 billion in assets that provides diversified financial services through its subsidiaries. Through Pioneer Bank, National Association and related units, it operates 22 offices in New York’s Capital Region, offering banking, insurance, employee benefits, human resources consulting, and wealth management services to individuals, businesses, and municipalities.

The company also owns Pioneer Capital Markets, Inc., a FINRA-registered broker-dealer focused on municipal bond trading. This mix of traditional banking, advisory, and capital markets capabilities positions Pioneer as a regional financial services platform serving a broad client base across the Capital Region and adjacent markets.

Endeavour Silver ( (TSE:EDR) ) has provided an update.

Endeavour Silver has filed a Form 6-K for April 2026 and notified investors that its annual general meeting of shareholders will be held on June 2, 2026, in Vancouver. The company is using Canadian notice-and-access provisions to deliver the notice of meeting, management information circular and related proxy materials electronically, aiming to cut paper use and reduce printing and mailing costs.

At the June 2, 2026 meeting, shareholders will be asked to receive the audited financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2025, set the board size at eight, elect eight directors and reappoint KPMG LLP as auditor. Only shareholders of record as of April 10, 2026 are eligible to vote, with detailed proxy and voting deadlines outlined to support orderly governance and shareholder participation.

An update from Horizon Bancorp ( (HBNC) ) is now available.

Horizon Bancorp, Inc. on April 22, 2026 reported unaudited first quarter 2026 results that underscored resilient profitability, with annualized return on average assets of 1.62%, return on average tangible common equity of 19.02% and net interest margin of 4.29%, while net income reached $26.2 million, or $0.51 per diluted share, slightly below the prior quarter but above the year-ago core run rate. The quarter featured robust core deposit growth of 11.3% annualized, driven by 22.8% annualized growth in non-interest-bearing deposits, modest 4.0% annualized commercial loan growth, strong credit quality with net charge-offs at 0.05% of average loans, and tightly managed expenses at $40.7 million, reinforcing management’s message of stable performance and solid capital generation despite a volatile macroeconomic backdrop.

Horizon’s balance sheet metrics highlighted ample funding for future loan growth, as elevated interest-earning cash balances from strong deposit inflows modestly compressed net interest margin by about four basis points in the quarter, reflecting a strategic decision to preserve pricing discipline rather than chase lower-yielding residential mortgages. With book value per share continuing to advance and core profitability remaining in the top tier of peers, the results suggested the bank is sustaining its competitive positioning in community banking while maintaining flexibility to support disciplined asset growth and long-term shareholder returns.

data.title: Pathward Financial Posts Solid Q2 Results Amid Tax Strength
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Pathward Financial ( (CASH) ) has provided an announcement.

Pathward Financial, Inc., a U.S.-based financial holding company focused on tax services, partner solutions and commercial finance, reported fiscal second-quarter 2026 net income of $72.9 million, or $3.35 per share, for the three months ended March 31, 2026, slightly down from $75.0 million a year earlier. Management highlighted strong tax-season performance, robust partner pipeline and growing commercial finance net interest income, while Pathward, N.A. again earned Great Place To Work certification, underscoring its emphasis on culture.

For the 2026 fiscal second quarter, total revenue rose to $276.3 million, driven by a 9% increase in noninterest income, with notable gains in card and deposit fees, tax-related fee income and refund transfer product fees, which together made noninterest income 55% of revenue. New loan originations excluding tax services climbed to $1.31 billion, supported by a 2025 consumer finance contract, and the company posted a 3.56% annualized return on average assets and 54.41% return on average tangible equity.

During the six months ended March 31, 2026, tax-services product revenue increased 13% to $95.7 million, with higher refund advances and transfers and a $4.4 million reduction in credit-loss provisions, lifting net tax-services product income 30% to $62.0 million; refund advance originations reached $1.87 billion versus $1.66 billion in the 2025 season. These gains reflect efforts to grow tax products, capture market share and refine underwriting models and data analytics in this core business line.

Net interest income for the quarter fell 8% to $125.1 million, mainly due to lower income from the consumer finance portfolio and cash, following the October 2025 sale of a consumer portfolio that had carried higher reported yields, while commercial finance loans and leases generated an $8.4 million interest-income increase. Average interest-earning assets slipped to $7.65 billion as the balance sheet shifted away from cash and securities toward loans and leases, and net interest margin narrowed to 6.63%, or 5.32% on an adjusted basis excluding gross consumer finance yields, with funding costs remaining low.

Noninterest income rose 9% to $151.2 million, aided by stronger tax-related fees, card and deposit fees and refund transfer revenues, partially offset by weaker secondary market and rental income after a prior-year working-capital portfolio gain. Servicing fees on custodial deposits at partner banks increased both sequentially and year over year on higher average balances, underscoring the importance of Pathward’s partner-bank arrangements and deposit-driven revenue streams.

Noninterest expense declined 3% to $143.5 million, reflecting lower card processing and other costs, even as the company increased spending on compensation, benefits, buildings and software to support its long-term strategy. Card processing expense, which is tied to rate-indexed partner agreements and federal funds rate moves, remained significant but decreased year over year, and the company’s effective tax rate for the quarter was 16.2% on income tax expense of $14.2 million, reinforcing solid bottom-line profitability despite portfolio repositioning.

The most recent analyst rating on (CASH) stock is a Buy with a $107.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Pathward Financial stock, see the CASH Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CASH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CASH is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong underlying fundamentals (high profitability and strong returns with currently low leverage) and positive technical momentum with the stock trading above key moving averages. A raised FY2026 EPS guide and improving operating metrics further support the outlook, while historical free-cash-flow inconsistency and execution/timing risks (partner ramp and tax season) temper the score.

To see Spark’s full report on CASH stock, click here.

More about Pathward Financial

Pathward Financial, Inc., listed on Nasdaq as CASH, is a U.S.-based financial holding company focused on powering financial inclusion through a mix of tax services, commercial and consumer finance, card and deposit solutions, and partner-driven banking platforms. Its subsidiary Pathward, N.A. operates as a nationally chartered bank, and the group emphasizes partner solutions, higher-returning assets, and workplace culture, having been Certified by Great Place To Work for four consecutive years.

Average Trading Volume: 267,805

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $2.17B

OIO Group ( (OIO) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 21, 2026, OIO Group received approval from Nasdaq for the anticipated closing of its previously announced business combination with De Tomaso Automobili Holdings Limited and the listing of the combined company on the Nasdaq Capital Market, subject to customary conditions. The company plans to consummate the deal around April 24, 2026, when the merged entity is expected to begin trading under the ticker OIO, supported by a 1-for-3 reverse stock split designed to satisfy Nasdaq’s minimum bid price and other initial listing requirements.

The reverse stock split will take effect immediately before the business combination closing, consolidating every three outstanding ordinary shares into one and forming a key part of OIO Group’s transaction and listing structure. Alongside assurances that its shareholder distribution will meet Nasdaq criteria, the company has published unaudited pro forma condensed combined financial information to illustrate the post-merger financial profile, signaling a significant step in its transition to a restructured, Nasdaq-compliant issuer.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

BCE ( (TSE:BCE) ) has provided an update.

BCE Inc. reported on April 21, 2026 the results of shareholder elections to convert between its fixed-rate Series AG and floating-rate Series AH preferred shares, effective May 1, 2026. After these conversions, the company will have 9,375,684 Series AG shares and 2,832,114 Series AH shares outstanding, both continuing to trade on the Toronto Stock Exchange.

For the five-year period starting May 1, 2026, Series AG preferred shares will pay a fixed quarterly dividend at an annual rate of 5.30%, while Series AH will maintain a monthly floating dividend tied to the prime rate and an adjustment factor linked to market pricing. The announcement clarifies BCE’s future preferred share capital structure and dividend terms, providing income-oriented investors with transparent options between fixed and floating yield exposure.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:BCE) stock is a Buy with a C$44.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on BCE stock, see the TSE:BCE Stock Forecast page.
